Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 214 upvotes, 14 comments
  2. Human Organ Atlas 53 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 65 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Rack-mount hydroponics 277 upvotes, 63 comments
  5. SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads 132 upvotes, 26 comments
  6. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 62 upvotes, 16 comments
  8. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 47 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 69 upvotes, 19 comments
  10. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 170 upvotes, 61 comments
  11. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 115 upvotes, 42 comments
  12. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 48 upvotes, 14 comments
  13. Trust no one: are one-way trusts one way? 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Pentagon expands oversight of Stars and Stripes, limits content 125 upvotes, 50 comments
  15. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 253 upvotes, 113 comments
  16. Centuries of selective breeding turned wild cabbage into different vegetables 106 upvotes, 42 comments
  17. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 41 upvotes, 13 comments
  18. Kniterate Notes 26 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 199 upvotes, 106 comments
  20. Palantir defends its role in the kill chain: "We are proud of that" 30 upvotes, 11 comments
  21. The Official DR DOS Website 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  22. The Appalling Stupidity of Spotify's AI DJ 333 upvotes, 259 comments
  23. Codegen is not productivity 27 upvotes, 13 comments
  24. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 31 upvotes, 16 comments
  25. How kernel anti-cheats work 289 upvotes, 239 comments
  26. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 277 upvotes, 231 comments
  27. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 52 upvotes, 40 comments
  28. Learning Athletic Humanoid Tennis Skills from Imperfect Human Motion Data 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Zipp 2001 Restoration 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  30. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 149 upvotes, 197 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
